Nevada · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) Guide

Issued by the Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health — Dietitian Licensing under the Department of Health and Human Services. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$237.50
$200 initial application fee + $37.50 fingerprint background check fee
Renewal
$200
Every 2 years (no state-mandated CE, but CDR requires 75 CPEUs per 5 years)
Processing Time
4 to 6 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health — Dietitian Licensing

Mailing Address

4150 Technology Way, Suite 300
Carson City, NV 89706

4150 Technology Way, Suite 300
Carson City, NV 89706

Phone & Fax

Fax: (775) 684-1073

dpbh@dhhs.nv.gov

Credential Categories

Nevada Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

The credential to practice dietetics in Nevada, regulated by the Division of Public and Behavioral Health.

Provisional Dietitian License

Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $200, and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

Temporary Dietitian License

Available for out-of-state dietitians practicing in Nevada for a short term. Valid for up to 6 months and costs $25.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Nevada Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of supervised practice.

Pass the CDR Examination

Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

Submit the Application via Nevada eLicensing

Create an account on the Nevada online portal, complete the application, and pay the $200 initial application fee.

Complete Background Check & Provide Documents

Submit fingerprints to the Central Repository for Nevada Records of Criminal History ($37.50 fee). Upload a copy of your current CDR registration card and official transcripts.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration CardProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Official College TranscriptsSent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
  • Fingerprint Background CheckVerification sent directly from the Nevada Department of Public Safety.
  • Photo ID & Proof of CitizenshipValid government-issued photo identification and evidence of lawful presence.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Nevada offers a provisional license ($200 fee) for individuals who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but are waiting to take the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an active Nevada Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Applicants actively licensed in another state can easily apply for Nevada endorsement by providing verification of their current active license, submitting the standard application, paying the $200 fee, and completing the fingerprint background check.

Frequently Asked Questions

Nevada Dietitian FAQ

How much does a Nevada dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $200, plus a $37.50 fee for the fingerprint background check. The provisional license also costs $200.

When does the Nevada license need to be renewed?

Nevada dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

What are the continuing education requirements for Nevada?

Nevada currently relies on the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) for continuing education tracking. There are no additional state-mandated CE hours beyond the 75 CPEUs required every five years to maintain active CDR registration.
